tasks.dependencies: ACC-003; acquisition rationale; role/account ownership decisions
tasks.risks: Client confusion, delivery risk, duplicated reporting, missed revenue and unclear leadership accountability.
tasks.evidence: Approved transition plan with milestones, owners, risks, client/account impacts and reporting rhythm.
tasks.doneCriteria: Transition plan approved; open actions tracked; client/account/pipeline ownership mapped; risk register updated.
tasks.source: Project Dora: Define Data Addiction transition plan to Q4 FY26

tasks.title: Issue post-acquisition leadership expectations and KPIs
tasks.description: Define and issue role expectations, KPIs, accountabilities and reporting requirements for acquired leaders, using role-based language and linking to Simplyai's operating cadence.
tasks.whyItMatters: Acquired leaders need clear expectations without creating separate management systems or ambiguity about Simplyai leadership standards.
tasks.contributors: COO, CFO, CTRO, Pillar Leads
tasks.dependencies: ACC-004; target pillar/category model; role clarity register
tasks.risks: Unclear accountability, inconsistent performance management and weak integration into Simplyai cadence.
tasks.evidence: Expectation pack and KPI mapping for acquired leaders.
tasks.doneCriteria: Expectations issued; KPIs connected to reporting cadence; leaders know forums, metrics and decision rights.
tasks.source: Project Dora: formal performance expectations for acquired leaders

tasks.title: Map acquired-company pipeline to Simplyai account ownership
tasks.description: Review pipeline inherited or influenced by acquired entities and assign accountability to Simplyai revenue leaders, account owners and pillar leads.
tasks.whyItMatters: Pipeline must be accountable in HubSpot and revenue governance, not held in legacy spreadsheets or individual networks.
tasks.contributors: COO, Pillar Leads, Finance, Sales
tasks.dependencies: HubSpot data standards; account owner model; ACC-005
tasks.risks: Pipeline leakage, duplicate client engagement, poor forecast quality and unclear accountability.
tasks.evidence: Mapping register showing account, deal, owner, status, source entity and next action.
tasks.doneCriteria: All acquired pipeline is recorded in HubSpot or formally excluded with rationale; every opportunity has a Simplyai owner.
tasks.source: Project Dora: review pipeline with leaders and ensure accountability sits with SAI leaders

tasks.title: Rationalise client contracts, MSAs and SOW templates across entities
tasks.description: Create a contract rationalisation plan covering Data Addiction, Optivia, Quinticon and Simplyai client contracts, MSAs, SOWs, novations, new booking entity rules and forward contract templates.
tasks.whyItMatters: Contract inconsistency creates legal, billing, margin, operational and client confusion as the group moves toward one operating model.
tasks.contributors: CFO, CTRO, CEO, Delivery, Finance
tasks.dependencies: Entity strategy; Booking Entity decision; MSA transfer plan
tasks.risks: Incorrect contracting entity, billing errors, inconsistent liability terms and poor client handover.
tasks.evidence: Register of contracts/MSAs/SOWs by entity, owner, action required and target state.
tasks.doneCriteria: All active contracts are categorised; DA priority actions identified; forward template rules agreed.
tasks.source: Project Dora: contract consistency across company; MSA transfer discussions

tasks.title: Catalogue and rationalise legacy products, accelerators and IP
tasks.description: Build a register of legacy products, inherited assets, demos, accelerators and reusable IP from Simplyai and acquired businesses, then decide whether each should be retained, retired, repackaged, governed or archived.
tasks.whyItMatters: Legacy products can create support risk, confusing GTM messages and hidden maintenance obligations if not rationalised.
tasks.output: Legacy Products and IP Rationalisation Register
tasks.contributors: COO, Pillar Leads, Partner Lead, Delivery
tasks.dependencies: Innovation Hub governance; acquisition asset discovery
tasks.risks: Unsupported products, duplicated assets, IP confusion, inconsistent client positioning and technical debt.
tasks.evidence: Register with owner, status, risk, client relevance, reuse potential and decision for each asset.
tasks.doneCriteria: All known legacy products/assets are captured; decisions are made; retained items have owners and governance.
tasks.source: Project Dora: plan for legacy products

tasks.title: Create acquisition integration reporting pack
tasks.description: Create a recurring pack showing integration milestones, pipeline integration, people transition, contract rationalisation, partner agreement status, revenue reporting readiness, risks and decisions required.
tasks.whyItMatters: The executive team needs one view of integration progress, not disconnected updates by function.
tasks.contributors: CFO, CTRO, People, Pillar Leads
tasks.dependencies: ACC-003; source data from HubSpot, Xero, People and contract registers
tasks.risks: Integration progress becomes anecdotal and risks surface late.
tasks.evidence: Pack with status, blockers, metrics, risks, decisions and owner actions.
tasks.doneCriteria: Pack reviewed in agreed forum; overdue items visible; decisions logged.
tasks.source: Project Dora acquisition reporting themes

tasks.title: Confirm service line, product, vendor and pillar revenue taxonomy
tasks.description: Define the official taxonomy for service lines, product/offering tags, vendor tags, pillar reporting and location/function tags across HubSpot, Xero, Astute, Employment Hero and Power BI.
tasks.whyItMatters: A shared taxonomy is essential for trusted segmented revenue, margin, utilisation, skills and pipeline reporting.
tasks.contributors: COO, CTRO, Pillar Leads, Systems Owner
tasks.dependencies: ACC-002; HubSpot data standards; Xero reporting requirements
tasks.risks: Multiple systems will produce different views of the business.
tasks.evidence: Approved taxonomy and mapping rules for each system.
tasks.doneCriteria: Taxonomy approved; fields mapped to HubSpot, Xero, Astute and Employment Hero; data owners assigned.
tasks.source: Project Dora: revenue categories, vendor allocation and post-acquisition reporting

tasks.title: Recode historical Xero invoices with service line and product tags
tasks.description: Complete controlled recoding of historical Xero invoices using agreed service line, product, vendor and professional services category tags, with QA checks and change log.
tasks.whyItMatters: Historical comparability is needed for credible FY26 reporting, margin analysis and board-ready trend reporting.
tasks.contributors: Commercial Analyst, RevOps Lead
tasks.dependencies: REV-001; Xero access; historical invoice data
tasks.risks: Incorrect historical revenue analysis and poor confidence in segmented reports.
tasks.evidence: Recoding log, QA sample, exception list and completion sign-off.
tasks.doneCriteria: All required invoices coded; exceptions documented; sample QA completed; reporting reconciles to Xero totals.
tasks.source: Project Dora: recode historical Xero invoices

tasks.title: Establish mandatory invoice coding policy going forward
tasks.description: Create and enforce mandatory invoice coding for Xero, including service line, product/offering tag, vendor tag, pillar/category, client, project/job code and coding owner.
tasks.whyItMatters: Prevents rework and keeps revenue, margin and service line reporting trustworthy from the point of billing.
tasks.contributors: Finance Manager, COO, RevOps Lead
tasks.dependencies: REV-001; finance process update
tasks.risks: Future reporting will degrade and finance will need recurring manual clean-up.
tasks.evidence: Approved policy, field list, owner model and monthly QA control.
tasks.doneCriteria: Policy approved; mandatory fields agreed; finance team uses it for all new invoices; monthly exception report exists.
tasks.source: Project Dora: mandatory invoice coding policy

tasks.title: Build segmented revenue and gross margin report
tasks.description: Build a report that shows revenue, gross margin and relevant cost indicators by service line, product/offering, vendor, pillar/category, client and entity where required.
tasks.whyItMatters: Leaders need to see where growth and margin are actually coming from, especially post-acquisition.
tasks.contributors: COO, CTRO, RevOps Lead, Power BI Owner
tasks.dependencies: REV-001, REV-002, REV-003; data access
tasks.risks: Leaders make decisions from anecdote rather than reliable segment economics.
tasks.evidence: Monthly report reconciled to Xero and included in management pack.
tasks.doneCriteria: Report produced monthly; reconciles to finance; used in MBR/QBR; includes commentary and exceptions.
tasks.source: Project Dora: build segmented revenue report

tasks.title: Document GL, job code and professional services category mapping
tasks.description: Document the GL accounts, job codes, project codes and professional services categories used to connect HubSpot, Xero, Astute and project profitability reporting.
tasks.whyItMatters: The reporting architecture must be visible and controlled, not implicit in individual spreadsheets or finance memory.
tasks.contributors: Systems Owner, COO, Commercial Analyst
tasks.dependencies: Xero setup; Astute data; REV-001
tasks.risks: Data quality issues, reporting inconsistency and difficulty onboarding new finance or operations users.
tasks.evidence: Data dictionary showing fields, owners, allowed values, source systems and reporting usage.
tasks.doneCriteria: Dictionary approved; stored in SharePoint; referenced by finance, RevOps and reporting owners.
tasks.source: Project Dora notes: revenue & COGS mapping complete; GL accounts set up

tasks.title: Build weekly project profitability report using Astute timesheets
tasks.description: Build a dynamic project profitability view that uses Astute timesheets, role/grade cost model, Xero revenue/cost data and project/job codes to show margin, leakage and contractor economics.
tasks.whyItMatters: Project margin leakage needs to be visible before it appears as a late financial surprise.
tasks.contributors: Delivery Lead, Finance Manager, Commercial Analyst
tasks.dependencies: Astute compliance; REV-005; standard cost model
tasks.risks: Under-scoped work, margin leakage and contractor costs may not be detected early.
tasks.evidence: Report with project revenue, effort, cost, margin, leakage, contractor cost and exceptions.
tasks.doneCriteria: Report refreshes from agreed sources; reviewed in finance/delivery cadence; material variances have owners.
tasks.source: Project Dora: build project profitability report from weekly timesheets

tasks.title: Define standard cost model per role and grade
tasks.description: Define standard internal cost assumptions by role, grade, employment type and contractor category for use in pricing, project profitability and workforce planning.
tasks.whyItMatters: Consistent cost assumptions are needed for pricing discipline, margin reporting and hiring affordability analysis.
tasks.contributors: COO, Delivery, Pillar Leads
tasks.dependencies: Employment Hero data; salary/cost inputs; contractor rate data
tasks.risks: Incorrect margin decisions and inconsistent profitability analysis.
tasks.evidence: Approved cost model with review date, assumptions and owner.
tasks.doneCriteria: Model approved; used in pricing, project profitability and workforce planning; assumptions reviewed quarterly.
tasks.source: Project Dora: define standard cost model per role/grade

tasks.title: Embed New Opportunity equals HubSpot Deal rule
tasks.description: Make it a formal operating rule that any qualified new opportunity requiring tracking, forecasting or presales support must be created as a HubSpot Deal.
tasks.whyItMatters: This protects HubSpot as the commercial source of truth and avoids shadow pipeline.
tasks.contributors: Sales, Presales, COO
tasks.dependencies: HubSpot mandatory fields and stage gates
tasks.risks: Pipeline leakage, duplicate requests and poor forecast visibility.
tasks.evidence: Updated sales process, HubSpot guidance and manager reinforcement.
tasks.doneCriteria: Sales leaders confirm rule; new Presales requests require Deal ID unless explicitly exempted.
tasks.source: Project Dora: New Opp = New Deal; HubSpot decision in prior chat

tasks.title: Implement 60-day stale deal closed-lost review
tasks.description: Create a weekly or fortnightly review that identifies HubSpot Deals with no stage movement or meaningful activity for 60 days and either closes them lost or records an approved exception.
tasks.whyItMatters: Stale deals inflate pipeline, weaken forecast quality and reduce confidence in board reporting.
tasks.contributors: Sales Leaders, CFO, COO
tasks.dependencies: HubSpot activity and stage data
tasks.risks: Overstated pipeline and poor forecast confidence.
tasks.evidence: Report showing stale deals, owner action and exception reason.
tasks.doneCriteria: Every stale deal is actioned, closed lost or has a documented exception within the review period.
tasks.source: Project Dora: any deal that hasn't moved in 60 days put to closed lost

tasks.title: Complete HubSpot missing data field remediation
tasks.description: Identify Deals, Companies and Contacts missing mandatory fields and run a remediation sprint with Sales and RevOps.
tasks.whyItMatters: Mandatory fields are the foundation for forecasting, presales triage, capacity planning and board metrics.
tasks.contributors: Sales, Presales, COO, CFO
tasks.dependencies: HubSpot field standards
tasks.risks: Dashboards cannot be trusted and Presales requests cannot be prefilled accurately.
tasks.evidence: Data quality report with missing fields by owner, field and age.
tasks.doneCriteria: Critical mandatory fields exceed agreed completeness threshold; exceptions reviewed weekly.
tasks.source: Project Dora: add missing data fields

tasks.title: Add HubSpot pipeline KPIs to board reporting pack
tasks.description: Add pipeline quality, weighted pipeline, forecast category, stale deal count, mandatory field completeness, stage conversion and close-date movement to board/shareholder reporting inputs.
tasks.whyItMatters: Board reporting should show pipeline quality as well as pipeline volume.
tasks.contributors: CEO, COO, CFO, Board Reporting Owner
tasks.dependencies: HS-025; forecast definitions
tasks.risks: Board may see optimistic pipeline without data quality or conversion context.
tasks.evidence: Board pack includes agreed HubSpot pipeline KPIs and commentary.
tasks.doneCriteria: KPIs are defined, sourced from HubSpot, reviewed before board pack and trended over time.
tasks.source: Project Dora: report to board with pipeline KPIs

tasks.title: Create decision record for paused pipeline weighting changes
tasks.description: Document that simplification of pipeline stages/weightings, combining Closed Won with Work Started Awaiting PO, and redefining backlog are paused or require executive decision before implementation.
tasks.whyItMatters: Avoids unapproved changes to forecast, backlog and revenue governance definitions.
tasks.contributors: COO, RevOps Lead, Sales
tasks.dependencies: Current HubSpot pipeline definitions; Project Dora paused items
tasks.risks: Unapproved forecast definition changes could distort reporting and delivery planning.
tasks.evidence: Decision record showing current position, options, recommendation and decision owner.
tasks.doneCriteria: Paused items are explicitly captured; no HubSpot configuration change occurs without approval.
tasks.source: Project Dora: paused pipeline weighting changes

tasks.title: Confirm Xero as finance and invoice system of record with coding controls
tasks.description: Update the systems register and operating controls to identify Xero by name as the finance, invoicing, GL and revenue coding system of record.
tasks.whyItMatters: Generic finance-system language is not enough for execution; teams need to know where data lives.
tasks.contributors: Finance Manager, Systems Owner
tasks.dependencies: Existing finance system row; Project Dora Xero references
tasks.risks: Unclear ownership and inconsistent finance data controls.
tasks.evidence: Systems register identifies Xero, critical fields, controls and reporting outputs.
tasks.doneCriteria: Xero row updated; coding controls mapped; finance reporting owners confirmed.
tasks.source: Project Dora: Xero invoice recoding and coding policy

tasks.title: Confirm Astute as timesheet and utilisation system of record
tasks.description: Update the systems register and SOPs to identify Astute by name as the timesheet, billable hours and utilisation source of truth with mandatory daily logging.
tasks.whyItMatters: Utilisation and project profitability depend on timely, trusted effort data.
tasks.contributors: Delivery Leads, Finance, People
tasks.dependencies: Timesheet rules and project codes
tasks.risks: Poor utilisation reporting, delayed billing and weak project profitability reporting.
tasks.evidence: Systems register identifies Astute; SOP defines daily logging, approvals and exception handling.
tasks.doneCriteria: Astute named as source; daily logging rule documented; weekly exception report exists.
tasks.source: Project Dora: mandatory daily logging - Astute

tasks.title: Confirm Employment Hero as employee, FTE and certification data system of record
tasks.description: Use Employment Hero as the system or primary reference point for employee profile, FTE, role, function, location and certification data, with links to skills matrix where needed.
tasks.whyItMatters: People and certification data needs one controlled source to support capability planning and partner status.
tasks.contributors: Skills Matrix Owner, Pillar Leads, Partner Lead
tasks.dependencies: Employment Hero configuration; certification register
tasks.risks: People data duplication, incomplete certification visibility and poor workforce reporting.
tasks.evidence: Data model showing fields, owners, update process and access rules.
tasks.doneCriteria: Employment Hero fields agreed; certification data process defined; reporting connection understood.
tasks.source: Project Dora: certification data and employee register in Employment Hero

tasks.title: Create FTE/headcount register linked to Employment Hero
tasks.description: Create a current employee register using Employment Hero and any relevant Neo process material, capturing role, manager, function, location, service line, pillar and employment type.
tasks.whyItMatters: Workforce planning and financial reporting need a trusted headcount baseline.
tasks.contributors: CFO, Pillar Leads, Systems Owner
tasks.dependencies: Employment Hero data model; role register
tasks.risks: Leadership cannot trust headcount, cost or capability reporting.
tasks.evidence: Register with agreed fields, owner and update cadence.
tasks.doneCriteria: Register live; records reviewed; fields complete for all active staff.
tasks.source: Project Dora: employment register leveraging Neo and Employment Hero

tasks.title: Tag each FTE by service line, function, location and pillar
tasks.description: Apply agreed tags to each employee record so reporting can show capability, workforce cost, utilisation and certification coverage by service line, function, location and pillar.
tasks.whyItMatters: Accurate tagging connects people data with revenue, skills, utilisation and reporting architecture.
tasks.contributors: CFO, Skills Matrix Owner, Pillar Leads
tasks.dependencies: REV-001; Employment Hero data model
tasks.risks: People, revenue and utilisation reports cannot be compared reliably.
tasks.evidence: Completion report showing tag completeness and exceptions.
tasks.doneCriteria: All active employees have mandatory tags or approved exceptions; monthly QA exists.
tasks.source: Project Dora: tag each FTE by service line, function and location

tasks.workstream: Recruitment, Pipeline and Delivery Capacity Management
tasks.title: Formalise graduate programme structure and intake calendar
tasks.description: Define whether Simplyai will operate a graduate programme, including role types, intake timing, assessment process, academy alignment, mentoring model, utilisation ramp and budget approval.
tasks.whyItMatters: Graduate intake can support capability depth but must be planned against demand, mentoring capacity and margin impact.
tasks.output: Graduate Programme Plan and Intake Calendar
tasks.contributors: CFO, Pillar Leads, Delivery Leads
tasks.dependencies: Capability framework; DA integration; workforce demand forecast
tasks.risks: Graduate intake may create bench cost or poor experience if not linked to work and mentoring.
tasks.evidence: Plan with intake numbers, timing, owners, cost, training, mentor capacity and success measures.
tasks.doneCriteria: Executive approval obtained; intake calendar published; mentors and onboarding plan confirmed.
tasks.source: Project Dora: formalise graduate program structure and intake calendar

tasks.title: Define certification incentive policy and vendor certification priorities
tasks.description: Document certification reimbursement/incentive rules and priority certifications by vendor, role level, partner requirement and strategic capability area.
tasks.whyItMatters: Certification effort should support partner status, client credibility and capability planning rather than ad hoc individual preference.
tasks.output: Certification Incentive Policy and Priority Certification Map
tasks.contributors: CInO, CTO, Partner Lead, Pillar Leads, CFO
tasks.dependencies: Skills matrix; partner strategy; Employment Hero certification register
tasks.risks: Certification spend may not support strategic vendor status or capability needs.
tasks.evidence: Policy and priority map approved; stored in SharePoint; tracked in Employment Hero/certification register.
tasks.doneCriteria: Policy covers reimbursement rules, approved certifications, priority vendors, review cadence and owner.
tasks.source: Project Dora: certs paid for; priority certifications per vendor and role level

tasks.title: Create vendor-by-vendor strategy and GTM plans
tasks.description: Develop practical strategies and GTM plans for Microsoft, Salesforce, Databricks, AWS and GCP, including partner status, account alignment, certification requirements, co-sell motions and target offers.
tasks.whyItMatters: Partner activity must be deliberate and tied to pipeline, capability and market positioning.
tasks.contributors: CTRO, CTO, Pillar Leads, Sales

tasks.risks: Partner opportunities remain opportunistic and hard to measure.
tasks.evidence: Vendor plans with owner, target accounts, certifications, plays, pipeline and next actions.
tasks.doneCriteria: At least priority vendors have agreed plans, AE alignment, certification gaps and pipeline actions.

tasks.description: Confirm accountable Account Executive or partner owner for each strategic partner relationship and ensure this is visible in HubSpot and partner governance.
tasks.whyItMatters: Clear ownership prevents partner relationships and co-sell opportunities from drifting.
tasks.contributors: Sales Leaders, CInO, CTO
tasks.dependencies: Partner strategy; HubSpot partner fields
tasks.risks: Partner relationships lack accountability and opportunities are not converted into pipeline.
tasks.evidence: Register with partner, owner, exec sponsor, current status, next actions and target pipeline.
tasks.doneCriteria: All priority partners have named owner, exec sponsor and review cadence.
tasks.source: Project Dora: assign Account Executive required for each partner

tasks.description: Review partner agreements inherited or held across Data Addiction, Optivia, Quinticon and Simplyai and agree which should be transferred, replaced, retired or governed under Simplyai.
tasks.whyItMatters: Partner agreements should align to the target entity and current strategic partner model.
tasks.contributors: CFO, COO, Legal/Commercial Owner, Pillar Leads
tasks.dependencies: Entity strategy; acquisition integration; partner strategy
tasks.risks: Legacy partner agreements may create unclear obligations or missed partner benefits.
